subject: Social Science (History)
overall_learning_objectives: Critically examine the social structure of nineteenth-century Indian society
Analyze the causes and consequences of practices like child marriage and restrictions on widows
Evaluate the contributions and ideas of major social reformers
Assess the importance of women’s education in social transformation
Examine caste-based discrimination and reform movements against it
Analyze the role of print culture and public debates in spreading reform ideas
Evaluate the long-term impact of social reform movements on modern Indian society

lesson_plan.title: Social Structure in 19th Century India
topics_covered: Position of women and lower castes
student_activities: Analyze textbook excerpts describing social conditions
Discuss reasons why social reforms became necessary

lesson_plan.title: Practices Affecting Women
student_activities: Examine a historical case related to child marriage
Discuss the social and psychological effects of these practices

lesson_plan.title: Major Social Reformers and Their Ideas
student_activities: Compare two reformers and their ideas

lesson_plan.title: Women’s Education and Social Transformation
student_activities: Debate: Why was women’s education controversial in the 19th century?

lesson_plan.title: Caste System and Reform Movements
student_activities: Write a reflection on equality and social justice

lesson_plan.title: Role of Print Culture and Public Debate
topics_covered: Newspapers and books as tools of reform

lesson_plan.title: Impact of Reform Movements and Critical Review
topics_covered: Achievements and limitations of reform movements
student_activities: Participate in a panel discussion on social reform

teacher_tip: Encourage students to critically analyze social practices and compare historical reforms with present-day social issues such as gender equality and caste justice.
